plan.title: Build a first-class Prism UI control plane around the existing dashboard, with an overview landing surface, a plans view, an architecture graph explorer, shared cross-links, and final concept curation so the repo-native architecture reflects the new human-facing product.
plan.goal: Build a first-class Prism UI control plane around the existing dashboard, with an overview landing surface, a plans view, an architecture graph explorer, shared cross-links, and final concept curation so the repo-native architecture reflects the new human-facing product.

node.title: Milestone 0: Lock the Prism UI information architecture, route map, and reuse boundaries
node.summary: Decide the top-level shell, route semantics, page responsibilities, and what existing /dashboard behavior stays intact so the implementation has a stable contract before code starts moving.

acceptance.label: The route plan explicitly covers `/`, `/dashboard`, `/plans`, and `/graph` with shared navigation and preserved direct access to the existing dashboard surface
Ownership boundaries are explicit across prism-mcp routes and read models, the React app shell, and any new plan or graph page data contracts
The rollout rule is explicit: keep the current dashboard usable throughout and keep freeform graph editing out of the first release

node.title: Milestone 1: Generalize dashboard serving into a top-level Prism app shell
node.summary: Extend prism-mcp routing and asset serving so the browser lands on a Prism overview shell first, while the current dashboard remains a preserved destination instead of the only surface.

acceptance.label: HTTP routes serve a top-level Prism entrypoint plus stable subroutes for dashboard, plans, and graph without breaking existing `/dashboard` deep links
Existing dashboard bootstrap, summary, operations, and SSE behavior remain available behind the new shell instead of being replaced ad hoc
New shell-serving code stays modular, with dedicated router and asset ownership rather than growing facade files into mixed-purpose logic

node.title: Milestone 2: Introduce a routed frontend shell and shared client data layer
node.summary: Refactor the current React dashboard app into a top-level Prism client with navigation, route-aware layout, and reusable client-side data primitives instead of one monolithic screen component.

acceptance.label: The frontend has an app shell and navigation that can host Overview, Dashboard, Plans, and Graph as coherent sibling surfaces
Shared fetching, bootstrap, and SSE connection state live in reusable client infrastructure instead of being duplicated per page
The current dashboard UI survives as a dedicated page inside the new shell rather than being discarded as throwaway code

node.title: Milestone 3: Build the overview page as the human entrypoint
node.summary: Add a first-load Overview surface that answers what is happening in Prism right now and points users into dashboard, plans, and graph contexts immediately.

acceptance.label: Overview shows system health, active work, blocked plan signals, recent outcomes, and hot concepts strongly enough to orient a human on first load
Overview cards and summaries deep-link into dashboard operations, plan detail, and graph focus states instead of being dead summary widgets
Overview is independently shippable as the new landing page even if later surfaces are still maturing

node.title: Milestone 4: Build a plans view on native coordination and plan-runtime data
node.summary: Expose plans, blockers, readiness, claims, handoffs, validations, and outcomes through a human-facing plans page that reuses existing coordination queries rather than reconstructing plan state ad hoc.

acceptance.label: The Plans page can list plans, inspect one plan graph, and show node status, blockers, next actions, and recent execution evidence
Human intervention stays structured in the first release: notes, blockers, acceptance, and status affordances come before any freeform graph editing
The page is backed by explicit UI read models or query adapters for plan summary, plan graph, execution overlay, and next-node recommendations

node.title: Milestone 5: Define the concept-graph projection, zoom model, and overlay contract
node.summary: Decide the first-cut architecture explorer contract around subsystem-level semantic zoom, typed relations, and bounded overlays so the graph page is inspectable instead of noisy.

acceptance.label: The graph starts at subsystem or concept level instead of trying to render the whole repo graph at once
The first release contract covers semantic zoom, neighborhood expansion, evidence inspection, and typed relation rendering
Overlay priority is explicit: concept relations first, then plan touchpoints, health or risk, and active work; freeform graph editing stays out of scope

node.title: Milestone 6: Implement the architecture explorer baseline
node.summary: Ship the Graph page with concept-centered navigation, evidence inspection, pathfinding, and layout tuned for exploration rather than whole-graph spectacle.

acceptance.label: Users can start from a clean architectural graph, expand concept neighborhoods, inspect evidence, and follow links down toward code or supporting context
Layout and interaction support semantic zoom and neighborhood exploration without collapsing into unreadable hairballs
The Graph page is backed by explicit UI-facing graph read models or query adapters rather than raw MCP payload dumping

node.title: Milestone 7: Add cross-view linking and meaningful overlays
node.summary: Connect overview, dashboard, plans, and graph so each surface can highlight the same runtime work, affected concepts, and validation or risk context.

acceptance.label: Clicking a plan can highlight touched concepts, and clicking a concept can reveal relevant active plans or live work
The first overlay set includes plan touchpoints plus at least one of health or risk or active-work state, with clear toggle semantics
Dashboard, Plans, and Graph feel like one product surface with shared context and deep links instead of unrelated tools

node.title: Milestone 8: Dogfood, validate, and ship the Prism UI control plane safely
node.summary: Validate the new shell and pages end-to-end, rebuild and restart prism-mcp, dogfood the control plane on real work, and close obvious trust gaps before the UI becomes the default entrypoint.

acceptance.label: Release build, MCP restart, status, and health checks confirm the live daemon is serving the new UI surface correctly
Manual dogfooding covers landing on Overview, drilling into Dashboard, inspecting Plans, and using the Graph explorer on a real repo flow
Any missing trust signals or broken deep links found during dogfooding are fixed or explicitly tracked before rollout

node.title: Milestone 9: Update Prism concepts and relations to match the new UI reality
node.summary: Refresh the repo-native concept layer so Prism’s own architecture graph and memory describe the product as a multi-surface human control plane, not just a standalone dashboard.

acceptance.label: Existing dashboard concepts are updated or split so Overview, Plans, Graph, and shared UI shell responsibilities are represented accurately
New or updated concept relations capture how the UI surface connects architecture, intent, execution, and evidence
Repo-scoped concept and memory updates land only after the implementation is real enough that future agents can rely on them

node.title: Milestone 0: Lock the Prism UI information architecture, route map, and reuse boundaries
node.summary: Milestone 0 decisions locked: keep `/dashboard` as a preserved destination and make `/` the new Prism overview shell; add sibling routes `/plans` and `/graph`; keep existing `/dashboard/api/*` and `/dashboard/events` as the dashboard-owned transport; add a new top-level UI boundary in prism-mcp for shell and non-dashboard page routing rather than growing dashboard_router; split the frontend into an app shell plus page modules (Overview, Dashboard, Plans, Graph) and shared data modules for dashboard, plans, and graph reads. Freeform graph editing stays out of the first release.

acceptance.label: The route plan explicitly covers `/`, `/dashboard`, `/plans`, and `/graph` with shared navigation and preserved direct access to the existing dashboard surface
Ownership boundaries are explicit across prism-mcp routes and read models, the React app shell, and any new plan or graph page data contracts
The rollout rule is explicit: keep the current dashboard usable throughout and keep freeform graph editing out of the first release

node.summary: Milestone 1 is ready after the IA decision: implement a top-level Prism shell route and preserve the existing dashboard transport boundary under `/dashboard`.

acceptance.label: HTTP routes serve a top-level Prism entrypoint plus stable subroutes for dashboard, plans, and graph without breaking existing `/dashboard` deep links
Existing dashboard bootstrap, summary, operations, and SSE behavior remain available behind the new shell instead of being replaced ad hoc
New shell-serving code stays modular, with dedicated router and asset ownership rather than growing facade files into mixed-purpose logic

node.title: Milestone 1: Generalize dashboard serving into a top-level Prism app shell
node.summary: Completed the top-level Prism UI shell routing: prism-mcp now serves the shared SPA at `/`, `/dashboard`, `/plans`, and `/graph` through dedicated `ui_router` and `ui_assets` modules, while `/dashboard/api/*` and `/dashboard/events` remain stable dashboard-owned transport. Release build, daemon restart, and live HTTP checks passed.

acceptance.label: HTTP routes serve a top-level Prism entrypoint plus stable subroutes for dashboard, plans, and graph without breaking existing `/dashboard` deep links

node.summary: Completed the routed frontend shell and shared client data layer: the monolithic dashboard app is split into an app frame, route model, reusable dashboard bootstrap/SSE hook, dedicated dashboard page, overview page, and placeholder plans/graph pages without discarding the current dashboard surface.

acceptance.label: The frontend has an app shell and navigation that can host Overview, Dashboard, Plans, and Graph as coherent sibling surfaces

The current dashboard UI survives as a dedicated page inside the new shell rather than being discarded as throwaway code
